title: nasturtium gambelii
intro: Nasturtium gambelii, also known as the Gambel's nasturtium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, known for its bright yellow or orange flowers and edible leaves and stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, with medicinal properties that include treating digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections. The leaves and flowers of Nasturtium gambelii can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, offering health benefits that include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
